As @WHO Regional Director for Africa, I am deeply concerned by the recent incident at the Ebola treatment centre in Bunia, which was inaugurated by Director-General @DrTedros and rehabilitated to an 80-bed capacity.  I was physically present during its inauguration, underscoring the importance of this facility for the people of Ituri.  The response efforts are led by the Government of the DRC, with support from WHO and partners, to deliver urgent services including safe and dignified burials. I urge communities to refrain from such actions and allow health workers and facilities to operate safely.  WHO will continue engaging communities through traditional, faith, women, and youth leaders, as well as local NGOs, to strengthen understanding of Ebola’s signs, symptoms, and prevention methods. ----------------------------------------------------  En tant que Directeur régional de l’OMS pour l’Afrique, je suis profondément préoccupé par l’incident récent survenu au centre de traitement d’Ebola à Bunia, inauguré par le Directeur général Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus et réhabilité pour une capacité de 80 lits.  J’étais physiquement présent lors de son inauguration, ce qui souligne l’importance de cette structure pour les populations de l’Ituri. Les efforts de riposte sont dirigés par le Gouvernement de la RDC, avec l’appui de l’OMS et des partenaires, afin de fournir des services urgents, y compris des enterrements sûrs et dignes.   J’exhorte les communautés à s’abstenir de tels actes et à permettre aux agents de santé et aux structures de fonctionner en toute sécurité. L’OMS continuera à mobiliser les communautés par l’intermédiaire des leaders traditionnels, religieux, des femmes, des jeunes et des ONG locales pour renforcer la compréhension des signes, symptômes et méthodes de prévention de l’Ebola. ----------------------------------------------------  Como Diretor Regional da OMS para a África, estou profundamente preocupado com o recente incidente no centro de tratamento de Ébola em Bunia, inaugurado pelo Diretor‑Geral Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus e reabilitado para uma capacidade de 80 camas.   Estive fisicamente presente durante a inauguração, o que demonstra a importância desta unidade para o povo de Ituri. Os esforços de resposta são liderados pelo Governo da RDC, com o apoio da OMS e parceiros, para fornecer serviços urgentes, incluindo enterros seguros e dignos. Apelo às comunidades que se abstenham de tais ações e permitam que os profissionais de saúde e as instalações funcionem em segurança.  A OMS continuará a envolver as comunidades através de líderes tradicionais, religiosos, mulheres, jovens e ONGs locais para reforçar a compreensão dos sinais, sintomas e métodos de prevenção do Ébola. ----------------------------------------------------  Kama Mkurugenzi wa Kanda wa WHO Afrika, nina wasiwasi mkubwa kuhusu tukio lililotokea hivi karibuni katika kituo cha matibabu ya Ebola huko Bunia, ambacho kilizinduliwa na Mkurugenzi Mkuu Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus na kurekebishwa kwa uwezo wa vitanda 80.   Nilihudhuria kwa mwili wakati wa uzinduzi wake, jambo linaloonyesha umuhimu wa kituo hiki kwa watu wa Ituri. Juhudi za kukabiliana zinaongozwa na Serikali ya DRC, kwa msaada wa WHO na washirika, ili kutoa huduma za dharura ikiwemo mazishi salama na yenye heshima.   Nawasihi jamii kuepuka vitendo kama hivyo na kuruhusu wahudumu wa afya na vituo kufanya kazi kwa usalama. WHO itaendelea kushirikisha jamii kupitia viongozi wa jadi, viongozi wa dini, wanawake, vijana na mashirika ya kiraia ya ndani ili kuimarisha uelewa wa dalili, ishara na mbinu za kuzuia Ebola. ----------------------------------------------------  Lokola Mokambi ya Région ya OMS na Afrika, nazali na posa makasi mpo na likambo oyo esalemi na centre ya kobikisa Ebola na Bunia, oyo ezwaki ouverture na Directeur Général Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus mpe ezongisamaki na capacité ya lits 80.   Nazalaki na présence physique na ouverture wana, elakisi ntina ya centre oyo mpo na bato ya Ituri. Misala ya kobunda ezali kosalelama na Gouvernement ya RDC, na lisungi ya OMS mpe ba partenaires, mpo na kopesa ba services ya urgent, na kati na yango kobakisa ba enterrements ya sécurité mpe ya lokumu. Nazali kosenga na ba communautés ete batika ba actions ya ndenge wana mpe bapesa nzela na ba agents ya santé mpe ba structures mpo esalaka na sécurité.   OMS ekobanda kaka kosangana na ba communautés na nzela ya ba leaders traditionnels, ba leaders ya nzambe, ba femmes, ba jeunes mpe ba ONG locales mpo na kobakisa mayele ya Ebola — ba signes na yango, ba symptômes mpe ba méthodes ya prévention.

Leith Walk. Edinburgh. Friday evening. A topless man storming up a busy thoroughfare carrying what appears to be a knife. The whole area now locked down large cordon, buses diverted, paramedics treating a man on the ground. And someone on a phone capturing it while swearing in disbelief.  The camera footage is the defining feature of modern Britain now. Every incident, every blade, every body on the pavement someone's filming it, and the commentary's always the same. "Oh my days, holy shit." Shock, but not surprise. Horror, but not confusion. Because everyone knows what they're looking at now. Another Friday night. Another busy street. Another man with a knife.  And look at the geography. This isn't an alley at midnight. It's not a backstreet in a known trouble spot. It's Leith Walk a main artery of Edinburgh, a city that's supposed to be one of the safer ones. 3:40am attacks, broad daylight stabbings, and now Friday evening on a road packed with people heading home, going out, living their lives all of it interrupted by a topless man with a blade.  The cordon's up. The buses have been diverted. The emergency response is in full swing, as it always is. But the question nobody answers is the same one every time: what's happening before the cordon? What's happening before the video? Because by the time someone's storming down Leith Walk half naked with a knife, the system's already failed.  Edinburgh. Friday night. Another one for the collection.  #LeithWalk #Edinburgh #KnifeCrime #Cordon #LothianAndBorders #Britanistan #NotTheEveningNews #TruthMatters #BrokenBritain #UKCrime #PublicSafety

You should read “Destroying America: A Dossier on the CIA’s Control of the Government.”  A car full of Secret Service agents did nothing to protect President Kennedy, and here’s why.  Official documents state that CIA officers are “detailed by the CIA” to be Secret Service agents and that the CIA first began doing it in 1955.  Four of the eight Secret Service agents in the Presidential follow-up car when Kennedy was assassinated were CIA officers who had been “detailed by the CIA” to the Secret Service.  (The Presidential follow-up car, or Secret Service follow-up car as it is sometimes known, rides directly behind the Presidential limousine when the President travels.)  The highest-ranking Secret Service agent in the follow-up car was a CIA officer named Emory Roberts, who rode in the front passenger seat next to the driver, Agent Samuel Kinney. Roberts had the prime position for watching the assassination unfold, as President Kennedy was directly in front of him in the rear seat of the Presidential limousine.  His three CIA colleagues in the Secret Service follow-up car were Special Agents Glenn Bennet, Tim McIntyre, and George Hickey. Bennett and Hickey rode in the rear seat of the follow up car, and McIntyre rode on the left rear running board next to Hickey.  In CIA officer Emory Roberts’ official report, he wrote that after a bullet struck President Kennedy in the head, he picked up the car radio and told Special Agent Lawson in the lead car, “The President has been hit. Escort us to the nearest hospital fast but at a safe speed.”  Roberts “repeated the message, requesting to be cautious, meaning the speed. I had in mind Vice President Johnson’s safety,” and Roberts added “as well as the President’s, if he was not already dead.” Roberts then “turned around to wave the Vice President’s car to come closer.”  “I said, pointing to McIntyre, ‘They got him, they got him,’ continuing I said, ‘You, meaning McIntyre, and Bennett take over Johnson as soon as we stop.’”  Roberts did not punctuate his Secret Service report with exclamation points, but he was most definitely making an exclamatory statement when he explicitly told McIntyre, “They got him! They got him!”  Roberts wrote that at 12:30 p.m., before witnessing the fatal head shot, he witnessed the “first of three shots fired, at which time I saw the President lean toward Mrs. Kennedy.”  With Special Agent John Ready standing just inches away on the right front running board, CIA officer Emory Roberts clearly saw that “they got him” with the “first” shot, and he clearly saw the President react to the shot, but according to his own Secret Service report, Roberts, the highest-ranking agent in the car, sat there and watched silently for at least five seconds and possibly as many as eight seconds while President Kennedy was being shot to death directly in front of him.  Roberts said and did absolutely nothing until President Kennedy was shot in the head.  (There is an ongoing debate concerning the amount of time it took to fire all the shots at President Kennedy, but the fact is it took at least five seconds and possibly as many as eight seconds.)  Senator Ralph Yarborough of Texas, who was riding with Vice President Johnson directly behind the Presidential follow-up car, could plainly see the reactions of Secret Service agents during the assassination. Yarborough’s affidavit to the Warren Commission states that when he heard the first shot, he “thought immediately that it was a rifle shot.”  Yarborough’s affidavit also states, “All of the Secret Service men seemed to me to respond very slowly, with no more than a puzzled look. In fact, until the automatic weapon was uncovered, I had been lulled into a sense of false hope for the President’s safety by the lack of motion, excitement, or apparent visible knowledge by the Secret Service men that anything so dreadful was happening.  “Knowing something of the training that combat infantrymen and Marines receive, I am amazed at the lack of instantaneous response by the Secret Service when the rifle fire began.”  Senator Yarborough was actually witnessing the result of the CIA sabotaging Presidential protection. The CIA officers, one of whom was running board agent Tim McIntyre, knew that there would be a problem if the other three running board agents, Landis, Ready, and Hill, took action to protect the President when the gunfire began.  Kinney, as a driver, certainly could not do anything during the assassination, but if it was going to be a successful assassination, the CIA officers would have to do something about the other three running board agents.  McIntyre’s partner, Glen Bennett, took decisive action to make sure the three agents would not be a problem.  Warren Commission Exhibit 1020 contains a news article stating that Secret Service agents were “in the Fort Worth Press Club the early morning of Friday, November 22, some of them remaining until nearly 3 a.m. . . . . They were drinking. One of them was reported to have been inebriated.”  It also states that after leaving the Press Club at “nearly 3 a.m.,” the Secret Service agents went to “an all-night beatnik rendezvous called ‘The Cellar.’”  Secret Service Chief James Rowley testified to the Warren Commission about the incident, stating, “There were nine men involved at the Press Club, and there were ten men involved at The Cellar.”  Rowley testified that Bennett and the three running board agents that needed to be disabled, Landis, Ready, and Hill, had participated in the drinking and late night activity.  Out of sixteen Secret Service agents in the Presidential motorcade, CIA officer Glen Bennett and the three running board agents were the only ones who participated in the drinking and late-night activity. The running board agents were supposed to be the first to shield the President from any possible danger.  The other five Secret Service agents who consumed alcohol at the Press Club were not in the Presidential motorcade on November 22, 1963.   Rowley claimed that someone, whom he did not identify, told the agents, “There was a buffet to be served at the Fort Worth Club,” but when the agents arrived, “there was no buffet.” Later in his testimony, he stated, “and they just thought while they were there, they would have a drink.”  Rowley glossed over it with one of three different versions of the drinking incident, testifying that he “ascertained in personal interviews” that three agents “had one scotch” and “others had two or three beers.” He also testified that agents “were in and out” of the Press Club from “roughly around 12:30 until the place closed at 2 o’clock.”  But in a letter to the Warren Commission several weeks prior to his testimony, Rowley put forth two versions of the drinking incident that were different from his Warren Commission testimony.  In one version, Rowley wrote that Calvin Sutton, president of the Press Club, had said that “the Press Club has a closing curfew of 12 midnight,” but Calvin Sutton, for some unknown reason, “kept the Press Club open until sometime after 2 a.m.,” which is clearly more than two hours past the “closing curfew.”  Sutton supposedly “ordered the bar at the Press Club closed” at “about 2 a.m.,” and “as the bar was closing, a party of about four people arrived who were later identified to him as Secret Service agents. Mr. Sutton requested the bartender serve them one drink, after which the bar was again closed and the party left.”  The other version in Rowley’s letter had at least something in common with his Warren Commission testimony. He wrote that what he determined “in the course of this investigation” was that “nine Special Agents of the White House Detail were in the Press Club at various times and departed at various hours up to 2 a.m.,” which is what he told the Warren Commission.  But Calvin Sutton, president of the Press Club, admitted that four Secret Service agents were there until sometime after 2 a.m. and it was reported that Secret Service agents were in the bar “until nearly 3 a.m.”  Rowley also claimed, “The amount of beer and liquor consumed by any of them did not exceed one and a half mixed drinks, or in one case, three glasses of beer,” which is different than his Warren Commission testimony that three agents “had one scotch,” and “others had two or three beers,” and it is completely different from the claim that “about four” Secret Service agents showed up at 2 a.m. and “Mr. Sutton requested the bartender serve them one drink.”  The claim that the bar was closing when about four agents got there and that it stayed open so they could have a drink is nowhere in his Warren Commission testimony. Also, his letter to the Warren Commission did not mention anything about Secret Service agents showing up for a buffet at 2 o’clock in the morning.  His letter did not say anything at all about a buffet, but it did say they were having a “party” at the Press Club, and after the bar finally closed, they obviously took the “party” over to the “all-night beatnik rendezvous,” otherwise known as The Cellar.  After testifying that the agents did not find a buffet and were “in and out” of the Press Club until it closed, Rowley stated, “After that, some of them went to The Cellar.”  CIA officer Glen Bennett and the three running board agents, Landis, Ready, and Hill, all admitted to consuming alcohol at the Press Club and then going to The Cellar afterward. As noted earlier, they were the only Secret Service agents assigned to the Presidential motorcade who participated in the drinking and late-night activity.  Rowley claimed they went to The Cellar after leaving the bar in the early morning hours of November 22 “out of curiosity, because this was some kind of a beatnik place.”  He acknowledged that “there was someone connected with the group who was intoxicated,” but he claimed that it was just someone that the agents “ran into” at the Press Club. He claimed the intoxicated man who was “connected with the group” was not a Secret Service agent.  CIA officer Tim McIntyre wrote in his report that Secret Service agents assigned to the Presidential follow-up car were working the “8 a.m. to 4 p.m. shift” on November 22, 1963.  Chief Justice Earl Warren asked Rowley when it comes to seeing someone with a rifle, “Don’t you think that if a man went to bed reasonably early and hadn’t been drinking the night before, he would be more alert to see those things as a Secret Service agent than if they stayed up until 3, 4, or 5 o’clock in the morning, going to beatnik joints and doing some drinking along the way?”  Rowley first tried to dodge the question and did not answer it, so Warren repeated, “I say, wouldn’t an alert Secret Service man in this motorcade, who is supposed to observe such things, be more likely to observe something of that kind if he was free from any of the results of liquor or lack of sleep than he would otherwise?”  And Rowley replied, “Well, yes; he would be.”  Warren also had Rowley read from the Secret Service manual, which strictly prohibits the consumption of alcohol, “including beer and wine, by members of the White House Detail and special agents cooperating with them, or by special agents on similar assignments, while they are in a travel status.”  All such agents “are considered to be subject to call for official duty at any time while they are in travel status . . . either during the day or night when they are off duty.”  The manual clearly reflects the seriousness of violating the prohibition against alcohol, stating, “Violation or slight disregard” of these regulations “at any time will be cause for removal from the service.”  As noted earlier, Landis, Ready, and Hill, being assigned to the outside running boards of the Presidential follow-up car, would be the first to react in the event of any danger to the President. They all had to wake up early, get ready, and report for the 8 a.m. shift, just a few short hours after their all-night partying with Bennett.  Bennett’s CIA colleague, Tim McIntyre, the fourth agent on the running boards, certainly did not need to be disabled. CIA officers McIntyre and Bennett were the agents that Roberts immediately assigned to “take over” Vice President Johnson after President Kennedy was shot in the head. That was when CIA officer Emory Roberts exclaimed, “They got him! They got him!”  Secret Service Chief Rowley wrote a letter to the Warren Commission stating, “The first duty of agents in the motorcade is to attempt to cover the President as closely as possible and practicable and to shield him by attempting to place themselves between the President and any source of danger.  “Agents are instructed that it is not their responsibility to investigate or evaluate a present danger, but to consider any untoward circumstances as serious and to afford the President maximum protection at all times.”  The running board agents’ Secret Service reports lay out in detail how they were unable to perform their “first duty” of affording the President “maximum protection at all times.”  Special Agent Landis’s report states that after he arrived at Dallas Love Field at 11:35 a.m., less than an hour before the assassination, he “walked to where the motorcade vehicles were parked.”  He then stood by the Presidential follow-up car and thought it would be funny to ask Special Agent Kinney, the agent who would be driving the car, where the follow-up car was. When the Presidential follow-up car “started moving,” Landis was standing “with my right leg on the running board and my left leg up and over and inside the follow-up car. I stayed in this position until we were leaving the airport area and remarked that, ‘I might as well get all the way in,’ and I did so.” (Landis arbitrarily decided that President Kennedy could make do with only three running board agents protecting him on November 22, 1963.)  Landis wrote that after he climbed all the way into the Presidential follow-up car, Roberts told him to “get back on the outside running board ‘just in case.’”  As the highest-ranking Secret Service agent in the car, CIA officer Emory Roberts certainly did not want to be scrutinized for allowing Landis to sit inside the car during the assassination. In the agents’ reports on the drinking incident, Landis admitted that he did not leave The Cellar, the all-night beatnik rendezvous, until “approximately 5:00 a.m.” But how much alcohol the agents actually consumed during their all night partying will never be known because the “Secret Service” is the only source for that information. A few seconds later, “the next shot was fired” and Landis “thought that maybe one of the cars in the motorcade had a blowout that echoed off the buildings,” and Landis “looked at the right front tire of the President’s car,” at which point Landis witnessed President Kennedy being shot in the head.  Special Agent Landis’s own report makes it clear that he was dazed and confused while the President was being assassinated. His report makes it clear that when he arrived at the Dallas airport less than an hour earlier, he was in no shape to perform his “first duty” of affording the President “maximum protection at all times,” not only because of the consumption of alcohol just hours earlier, but also because of a definitive lack of sleep.  CIA officer George Hickey arrived in Dallas the previous day on an Air Force plane transporting the Presidential limousine and the Secret Service follow-up car, and he admitted to being “an extra man” in the follow-up car. Another Secret Service report states that Hickey’s CIA colleague, Glen Bennett, was with the “Protective Research Section” and only “temporarily assigned to the White House Detail.”  Instead of having just two CIA officers on hand for the assassination, which would be Roberts and McIntyre, they were able to get Hickey and Bennett into the back seat and have four CIA officers in the follow-up car.  CIA officer Hickey wrote that Roberts instructed him to “take control of the AR-15 rifle” whenever he was riding “as an extra man” in the Presidential follow-up car. The AR-15 rifle was the “automatic weapon” that Senator Yarborough saw “uncovered” after witnessing “all of the Secret Service men” responding “very slowly, with no more than a puzzled look.”  Hickey wrote that it was not until “the end of the last report” that he “reached to the bottom of the car and picked up the AR-15 rifle, cocked and loaded it, and turned to the rear.”  Bennett, who was only “temporarily assigned to the White House Detail,” wrote in his “Protective Assignment” report that after seeing the last shot strike the President in the head, he “immediately hollered ‘he’s hit’” and then “reached for the AR-15 located on the floor of the rear seat. Special Agent Hickey had already picked-up the AR-15.”  But before seeing the last shot strike President Kennedy in the head and before saying anything, CIA officer Bennett watched silently as President Kennedy was shot in the back.  Bennett wrote that he “looked at the back of the President” and “saw the shot hit the President about four inches down from the right shoulder.” Even though Bennett saw the President take a bullet in the back, Bennett sat there and said absolutely nothing. It was not until one of the assassins’ bullets struck President Kennedy in the head that Bennett hollered, “He’s hit!”  After five to eight seconds of gunfire, President Kennedy sustained what proved to be a fatal head wound, at which point CIA officers Hickey and Bennett realized the plan had come to fruition.  Like CIA officer Roberts, they both had an instantaneous response. Hickey belatedly “picked up the AR-15 rifle” and “turned to the rear,” and Bennett exclaimed, “He’s hit!” and reached for the AR-15 rifle that Hickey was already holding.  Roberts then twice instructed the lead car to drive to the hospital “at a safe speed,” after which he turned to CIA officer McIntyre and exclaimed, “They got him! They got him!”  Hickey’s report states that he is with the “White House garage,” and McIntyre’s report describes Hickey as “a driver.” Special Agent Kinney drove the Presidential follow-up car, while Special Agent Greer drove the President’s limousine, and Texas State Highway Patrolmen were driving both the Vice President’s car and the Vice Presidential follow-up car.  CIA officer Hickey, the “driver” with the “White House garage,” had nothing to drive, which obviously made him an “extra man” in the follow-up car.  Hickey wrote that while President Kennedy was being treated at Parkland Hospital, Assistant Special Agent in Charge Roy Kellerman “told Agent Kinney and me to take the cars to the plane and stand by for orders.” Hickey then drove the Presidential limousine to the airport.  Hickey would have served no purpose in the Presidential follow-up car if President Kennedy had not been assassinated, but by tagging along as an “extra man,” Hickey was conveniently on hand to drive the President’s limousine back to the airport and thus have access to the crime scene.  The Warren Commission Report states, “After the Presidential car was returned to Washington on November 22, 1963, Secret Service agents found two bullet fragments in the front seat.”  “One fragment” was “found on the seat beside the driver,” and the “other fragment” was “found along the right side of the front seat.” The FBI “positively identified” both fragments as having been “fired” from the “rifle found in the Depository,” the building from which Lee Harvey Oswald allegedly fired the shots.  CIA officer Hickey had no problem whatsoever planting two bullet fragments that had at one time been fired from the rifle that would be “found in the Depository.”  The reactions of Secret Service agents in the Presidential follow-up car, as detailed in the reports they wrote, clearly contradict what they were expected to do, especially the reactions of CIA officers Roberts, Bennett, McIntyre, and Hickey.  The previously cited letter from Chief Rowley to the Warren Commission clearly explained that agents in the motorcade are instructed to “consider any untoward circumstances as serious” and “afford the President maximum protection at all times.”  CIA officer Roberts, who was sitting in the prime position for witnessing the assassination and who was the highest-ranking agent in the follow-up car, stated very clearly that he sat and watched everything from the first shot to the last without saying a word until it was all over.  As noted earlier, Special Agent John Ready, the agent closest in proximity to President Kennedy, was standing right next to Roberts on the right front running board while Roberts silently and patiently watched the CIA assassinate the President of the United States.  CIA officer Bennett’s report states that at the sound of the first shot, he looked at the President and watched everything without saying or doing anything until it was all over.  McIntyre, the only CIA officer on a running board, wrote that the President’s car was 200 yards from the underpass “when the first shot was fired,” and “after the second shot” he “looked at the President and witnessed his being struck in the head by the third and last shot.”  McIntyre did not say what he was doing for five to eight seconds after “the first shot was fired,” but a photo shown later in this chapter shows McIntyre focused across the Secret Service car on Special Agents Ready and Landis, two of the three running board agents that needed to be disabled.  CIA officer Hickey reported that he stood up and turned his back to the President at the sound of the first shot, allegedly “in an attempt to identify it,” and then after “two or three seconds” of looking toward the rear, he turned to look at the President and watched as the next two shots were fired.  Hickey picked up the AR-15 rifle only after a bullet struck President Kennedy in the head, more than five seconds and possibly as many as eight seconds after the first shot.  Every patriot should read “Destroying America: A Dossier on the CIA’s Control of the Government.”  amazon.com/dp/B0H3FJ7R1X

Utopianism.  For that sentiment, whether she recognizes it or not, undergirds her activism — the belief that in order for humanity to be "free," the Jewish State must be isolated & eventually removed from the face of the Earth  Hers isn't a naive call for a "two-state solution" or a recognition that both Israelis and Palestinians have rights to the land. No.  This is well beyond that.  Yet, one cannot solely blame Rooney for her rhetoric, for she exists with a vast ecosystem of hate within both Official Ireland and the broader Irish arts and literary community, which has, in the name of "social justice," succumbed to the darkest impulses of mankind  Make no mistake about it: the Nazis themselves believed they too were on the "right side of History"

Суд в Великобритании приговорил двоих осуждённых за поджоги дома премьер-министра к семи и двум годам лишения свободы. За поджогами стояла Россия, выяснили СМИ  О приговоре, вынесенном гражданину Украины Роману Лавриновичу и родившемуся в Украине гражданину Румынии Станиславу Карпюку, сообщает Би-би-си. Лавринович осуждён на семь лет лишения свободы, Карпюк — на два.  В понедельник присяжные признали их виновными в поджогах дома семьи премьера Великобритании Кира Стармера и другого связанного с ним имущества в мае прошлого года. Ещё один задержанный по этому делу — Пётр Починок — был оправдан.  Из материалов расследований The Financial Times и Би-би-си следует, что за этими поджогами стояла Россия. По данным следствия, как минимум Лавриновича завербовал в телеграме русскоязычный куратор под никнеймом El Money. FT пишет, что El Money находился в России и был тесно связан с NoName057(16) — прокремлёвской хакерской группой, которую в США считают «секретным проектом», связанным с Кремлём.  Би-би-си пишет, что куратора зовут Евгений Люкшин, это молодой российский дипломат и сын высокопоставленного чиновника. Pendant la réunion des leaders du G7 en France, une vague d’attaques informatiques a visé Évian-les-Bains et ses alentours. Lié aux services spéciaux, NoName057(16) a frappé des organismes à Thonon-les-Bains, Annecy et Saint-Gingolph.

According to statements circulating on Russian Telegram channels, the operation reportedly began on June 15 and allegedly involves automated tools designed to identify vulnerable surveillance devices, gain access, and collect video feeds.   The actors further claim that AI-powered analytics are used to process captured footage, extracting information such as faces, vehicle license plates, vehicle characteristics, and other observable details.  The claims suggest that collected information is indexed and stored alongside geolocation data for later analysis.  At the time of writing, there is no independent verification of the reported scale of the operation, the alleged compromise of 50,000 cameras, or the technical capabilities described by the actors.  If authentic, such activity would highlight the growing convergence of cyber operations, surveillance technologies, and AI-driven data analysis.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #Surveillance #AI #Europe #Ukraine #NoName0571

> "we now seem much closer to a world where models will be able to use off-the-shelf physical tools with relative ease... more research is needed to understand models’ ability to make these physical tools more bespoke, whether by writing control policies tailored to particular tasks or by designing robotic systems"  When this works, it will be bc frontier models can make api calls to other models trained on real-world data at scale. It won't be by writing control policies tailored to particular tasks.  Frontier models are obviously superhuman at code-gen, but code-as-policies has always been doomed. Not bc humans are 10x or 100x or 1000x too slow at writing good code, but bc software 1.0 is simply not the right action space for robotics. This is a key distinction b/w physical and virtual agents.

Beyond H-1Bs, there’s another threat to young Americans’ jobs: the OPT program.   The OPT program allows foreigners here on F-1 student visas to stay for three years after graduation and work.  Employers save 15.3% by hiring OPTs because they do not have to pay social security or medicare taxes OPTs. You can imagine how this hurts Americans workers.   There is no annual cap on the OPT program.  This is an unlimited source of cheap, foreign labor for employers.  ICE recently identified the OPT program as a magnet for fraud and has identified more than 10,000 potential cases of fraud in the OPT program at just the top 25 OPT employers in the country.  I oppose the OPT program because it undermines Americans who are starting their careers so they can get married, buy a home and raise a family in Utah.   Representative Eli Crane has proposed a bill called the End H-1B Visa Abuse Act of 2026. Among other things, this bill will end the OPT program. As your congressman, I will co-sponsor this bill and help get it passed into law.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Windows Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) Zero-Day Advertised for Sale  A threat actor is advertising an alleged Windows Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) zero-day exploit on an underground forum for $120,000.  According to the seller, the exploit:  * Escalates privileges to SYSTEM * Requires no additional software or dependencies * Allegedly works with or without EDR solutions * Targets Windows 10 and Windows 11 systems * Claims compatibility with Windows Server 2016 through current versions * Proof-of-concept reportedly available to vetted buyers  The actor is offering the vulnerability through an escrow-based transaction and claims broad compatibility across enterprise and consumer Windows environments.  Potential Risks:  * Full system compromise following initial access * EDR bypass attempts * Ransomware deployment * Credential theft and lateral movement * Persistence and privilege escalation in enterprise networks  Why This Matters:  * LPE vulnerabilities remain a critical component of modern attack chains, often used after phishing, malware execution, or initial foothold acquisition. * Threat actors increasingly seek reliable privilege escalation capabilities to bypass security controls and obtain SYSTEM-level access. * Even if a claimed exploit is exaggerated or fraudulent, advertisements like this provide insight into current underground demand and pricing for Windows exploitation capabilities.  Analyst Note: High-priced exploit advertisements should be treated cautiously until independent validation exists. However, the continued demand for Windows privilege escalation vulnerabilities highlights their importance in ransomware, espionage, and post-compromise operations across enterprise environments.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Windows

Vibe-coded security checklist:  * Passwords stored in localStorage ✅ * API keys in JavaScript ✅ * Admin panel exposed ✅ * Security review skipped ✅ * Electrical outlet inside the sink ✅  At least they're committed to innovation. 😂  #DDW #DarkWeb #Intelligence #CyberSecurity

🇺🇸 United States - GAP Inc. Customer Database Allegedly Leaked  A database allegedly associated with GAP Inc. has been shared on a hacking forum, exposing customer-related information and loyalty program data.  According to the threat actor, the dataset contains:  * 256,200 unique email addresses * 152,100 phone numbers * 146,100 home addresses * Customer account information * Loyalty program data * Additional personally identifiable information (PII)  Potential Risks:  * Targeted phishing campaigns against customers * Account takeover attempts leveraging exposed contact data * Identity theft and fraud * Loyalty account abuse and reward theft * Social engineering attacks using customer profile information * Increased credential stuffing activity if associated credentials are exposed elsewhere  Analyst Note: The exposed dataset appears to contain a significant amount of customer contact information alongside account-related records. While the authenticity and origin of the data require independent verification, threat actors frequently leverage retail customer datasets for phishing, fraud, and account recovery attacks due to the trust customers place in well-known brands.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #GAP

🇺🇸 United States - Associated.org The Associated (Jewish Federation of Baltimore) Allegedly Listed on Leak Site  The Associated: Jewish Federation of Baltimore, a nonprofit organization supporting Jewish community programs, education, advocacy, social services, and charitable initiatives, has been listed on a leak platform associated with the LockBit ransomware operation.  According to the posting:  * Organization: The Associated: Jewish Federation of Baltimore * Sector: Nonprofit / Community Services * Location: Baltimore, Maryland, USA * Threat Actor Attribution: LockBit * Status: Alleged data leak listing * Affected Domain Reference: jfbg.org  Potential Risks:  * Exposure of donor information * Member and volunteer data disclosure * Employee and contractor information exposure * Financial and operational document leakage * Reputational damage and targeted phishing campaigns * Increased risk to affiliated community organizations  Analyst Note: At the time of posting, no detailed description of the allegedly compromised files or records was publicly visible in the listing. However, nonprofit organizations often maintain sensitive donor, beneficiary, volunteer, and financial records that could be attractive targets for financially motivated threat actors. Independent verification is required to determine the scope and authenticity of any leaked data.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #LockBit

According to the threat actor, the leaked SQL database contains customer information including:  * Full names * Phone numbers * Addresses * Customer identification records * Additional account-related information  Leak Details:  * Format: SQL * Claimed Size: 369 MB * Distribution via public download link  Potential Risks:  * Targeted phishing and social engineering attacks * Identity theft and fraud attempts * Unauthorized contact campaigns * Credential reuse attacks if authentication data is present * Increased risk of scams targeting outdoor and e-commerce customers  Analyst Note: While the forum post provides only a limited description of the exposed fields, the combination of names, phone numbers, and physical addresses would present privacy and security concerns for affected customers if the dataset is authentic. Verification of the data and assessment of its freshness remain necessary before determining the full impact.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Russia

🇺🇸 United States - tastytrade Trading Platform Database Allegedly Exposed  A threat actor is advertising a database allegedly linked to tastytrade, a U.S.-based online brokerage and options trading platform.  According to the forum post, the exposed records reportedly contain:  * First and last names * Email addresses * Phone numbers * Physical addresses * City information * IP addresses * Dates of birth * Trading-related profile data * Options product information * Commission settings and limits * Customer ratings and account-related attributes  Potential Risks:  * Targeted phishing against investors and traders * Account takeover attempts through credential stuffing * Identity theft and financial fraud * Social engineering attacks using personal and trading profile data * Increased risk of investment-related scams  Analyst Note: The presence of personally identifiable information combined with brokerage-related account attributes could significantly increase the effectiveness of financial fraud campaigns. The authenticity and recency of the dataset should be independently verified before drawing conclusions regarding impact.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Tastytrade

🇶🇦 Qatar State Security Data Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor claims to have obtained and is selling sensitive data allegedly linked to Qatar State Security personnel.  According to the forum post, the exposed information reportedly includes:  * Full names and tribal affiliation * Mother's full name * National ID numbers * Passport numbers * Phone numbers * Dates of birth * Residential addresses * Educational certificates * Military ranks and status * Marital status * Number of children * Enlistment dates * Service expiration dates * ID issuance and expiration details  The seller claims the dataset was extracted from internal systems and advertises the data for sale for $5,000. The post references a data freshness date of May 2026.  Potential Risks:  * Intelligence targeting of government personnel * Identity theft and impersonation * Physical security risks to affected individuals * Nation-state espionage activity * Social engineering and recruitment targeting * Exposure of military and security-related personnel information  Analyst Note: If authentic, this would represent a significant exposure due to the sensitivity of the alleged records. Claims made by threat actors should be independently verified, but the combination of identity, military, and personal information would make such a dataset highly valuable for intelligence collection and targeted operations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Qatar

🇫🇷 French Real Estate Platform Database Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor has published a database allegedly associated with Ouestfrance-Immo.pro, a French platform serving real estate professionals and property marketing agencies.  According to the forum post, the dataset reportedly contains:  * 16,855 records * Information related to 12,648 real estate agencies * JSON-formatted data * Agency profiles and business information * Contact and listing-related records * Property marketing platform metadata  The seller shared sample records that appear to contain agency and platform-related information.  Potential Risks:  * Targeted phishing against real estate agencies * Business email compromise (BEC) attempts * Fraud involving property listings * Exposure of agency contact information * Supply chain and partner-targeted attacks  Why This Matters:  * Real estate organizations frequently handle sensitive customer, financial, and property transaction information. * Attackers often leverage agency contact databases to conduct highly targeted phishing and invoice fraud campaigns. * Even relatively small datasets can provide valuable intelligence for broader attacks against the real estate sector.  Analyst Note: Real estate platforms remain attractive targets because they connect thousands of agencies, brokers, and property professionals. Attackers can use exposed agency data to identify high-value targets and launch convincing sector-specific social engineering campaigns.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇮🇱 Atraf LGBTQ Dating Platform Database Allegedly Resurfacing on Underground Channels  A threat actor is advertising what is claimed to be the full database of Atraf, an Israeli LGBTQ dating platform, on an underground forum.  According to the post, the dataset allegedly contains:  * Approximately 835,000 user records * Full database archive * Compressed dataset (~780 MB) * User account information * Dating platform profile data  The forum post references a downloadable archive and promotes the dataset as a complete user database.  Potential Risks:  * Exposure of highly sensitive personal information * Doxxing and identity exposure * Blackmail and extortion attempts * Targeted phishing campaigns * Privacy and personal safety concerns  Why This Matters:  * Dating platform breaches often carry significantly higher risks than traditional consumer breaches due to the sensitivity of user information. * Individuals may face reputational, personal, or physical security concerns if profile data is exposed. * Historical dating-platform datasets frequently continue circulating years after the original compromise, increasing long-term risk for affected users.  Analyst Note: Breaches involving dating platforms should be treated as high-impact privacy incidents. Even when datasets are not new, their redistribution can expose users to renewed targeting, extortion attempts, and social engineering campaigns.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Israel

🇺🇸 U.S. Online Retailer Database Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising a database allegedly associated with ButtonMakers.net, a U.S.-based company specializing in button-making equipment and supplies.  According to the forum post and shared sample, the dataset may contain:  * User IDs * Email addresses * Password data * Account privilege levels * Registration timestamps * Customer account records * Order-related information  The seller claims the database originates from the company's online platform and provided sample records to support the listing.  Potential Risks:  * Credential stuffing attacks * Account takeover attempts * Customer phishing campaigns * Identity theft and impersonation * Unauthorized access to customer accounts  Why This Matters:  * Databases containing authentication-related information remain highly valuable in underground markets. * Even older customer records can be leveraged in large-scale credential reuse campaigns. * Small and medium-sized e-commerce businesses are increasingly targeted because attackers often expect weaker security controls and slower incident response.  Analyst Note: When password-related fields appear in leaked datasets, organizations should assume threat actors will attempt credential reuse attacks against other services. Affected users should reset passwords immediately and enable multi-factor authentication wherever available.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #USA

sample_tweets.text: 🔎 74,000 FortiGate records.  At first glance, it looked like another large credential exposure.  But ThreatMon's investigation revealed something more structured.  In our latest analysis, we examine what was exposed, what the infrastructure revealed, and why an adversary would invest resources into collecting 74,000 FortiGate-related records.  Read the full analysis to explore the findings and implications: threatmon.io/why-would-an-ad…

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Massive Naver Smart Store Merchant Data Leak  A darkweb forum post claims that a massive data breach has exposed personal and business information of Naver Smart Store merchants, allegedly including 732.000 email addresses and 1.420.00 phone numbers. According to the listing on an underground forum, the stolen data originates from sell.smartstore.naver.com, South Korea's leading e-commerce marketplace platform.  The post claims the breach includes comprehensive personally identifiable information (PII) and business details for merchants operating on the Naver Smart Store platform. The actor alleges the following data types were compromised: - First name and last name - Mobile phone numbers - Fax numbers - Email addresses - Date of birth - Physical addresses - Business registration numbers - Shopping categories - CEO names - Company addresses - Website URLs  A sample file attached to the post, named "sample_sell.smartstore.naver…_@Data1ortex_db.xlsx," displays 25 records of merchant data including company names, contact information, and business details. The data appears to represent individual sellers and small businesses operating storefronts on the platform.  Naver Smart Store is operated by Naver Corporation, one of South Korea's largest internet companies and a dominant player in the Korean digital marketplace. The platform enables individuals and businesses to create and manage online stores, serving as critical e-commerce infrastructure for hundreds of thousands of Korean merchants. If authentic, exposure of merchant PII and business registration data could enable targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and social engineering attacks against store operators.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of Naver Smart Store merchant data has not been independently verified.  #DataBreach #NaverSmartStore #ECommerceSecurity #SouthKoreaCyber #Data1ortex #PIIExposure #DarkWeb

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 8M Argentine Citizen Records on Darkweb Forum  A darkweb forum post reveals the alleged sale of a massive database containing personal information of over 8 million Argentine citizens. According to the listing, the data was scraped in May 2026 and covers individuals aged 18 to 70 across eight major provinces, including Ciudad de Buenos Aires, Catamarca, Córdoba, Corrientes, Entre Ríos, Jujuy, La Rioja, and Mendoza. The actor claims the dataset contains 8,175,364 unique rows and is being offered for 0.05 XMR per decryption key.  The post alleges that the database includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) and government-issued identifiers. The exposed data reportedly consists of: - DNI (National Identity Document) numbers - CUIT (Tax Identification Numbers) - Full names - Years of birth - Province and town of residence  To verify the legitimacy of the leak, the seller provides a sample zip file via Onionshare containing a redacted database, an encrypted vault file named vault.db, and a Python script titled restore_db.py. The actor claims that the redacted database allows buyers to cross-reference known DNI numbers, with names partially masked and birth years showing only the final digit. The accompanying Python script allegedly contains the decryption logic to restore the original database using the purchased key.  If authentic, the exposure of millions of Argentine tax and identity records could facilitate large-scale identity theft, financial fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns across the country. Argentina's Federal Public Revenue Administration (AFIP) and national civil registry databases have historically been targets for cybercriminals seeking bulk PII.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach and the authenticity of the 8.1 million records have not been independently verified.  #DataBreach #Argentina #PII #DarkWeb #DataSale #CyberSecurity #IdentityTheft

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 3.9M Record Leak of Vietnam ISP SCTV.VN  A darkweb forum post claims the exposure of a massive database belonging to SCTV.VN, a prominent internet service provider and cable television operator in Vietnam. The listing, posted by an actor using the handle "Orcinus orca," alleges the leak contains subscriber data for 3.9 million users.  According to the post, the compromised dataset includes sensitive customer information and technical identifiers. The actor claims the following data is exposed: - Full Personally Identifiable Information (PII) - ONT (Optical Network Terminal) serial numbers  SCTV is a major telecommunications entity in Vietnam, operating as a subsidiary of the Saigon Times Corporation. The exposure of 3.9 million records represents a significant breach of consumer privacy. Additionally, the inclusion of ONT serial numbers is notable; these identifiers are used in fiber-optic networks to authenticate customer premises equipment. If authentic, the exposure of these technical artifacts could enable precise targeting of the provider's infrastructure or facilitate advanced social engineering attacks against subscribers.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach of SCTV.VN has not been independently verified.  #DataLeak #SCTV #Vietnam #TelecomSecurity #DarkWeb #PII #Infrastructure

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 4.9M Wise User Records for Sale on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post alleges the sale of a database containing 4.9 million user registrations from Wise, the international money transfer service, specifically targeting customers in Spain. According to the listing, the archive purportedly includes records of individuals who have transacted on the platform's Spanish domain (wise.com/es).  The post claims the dataset contains 4.900.307 rows of highly sensitive personal and compliance data. The seller enumerates the following fields within the alleged dump: - id_usuario, timestamp_utc - perfil_personal.nombre, perfil_personal.apellidos, perfil_personal.fecha_nacimiento, perfil_personal.genero - documento_legal.tipo_doc, documento_legal.num_doc, documento_legal.pais_iso, documento_legal.estado_verificacion - canal_comunicacion.telefono.completo, canal_comunicacion.telefono.validado - canal_comunicacion.email.direccion, canal_comunicacion.email.entregable - meta_geografia.pais, meta_geografia.provincia, meta_geografia.localidad - registro_cumplimiento.estado_kyc, registro_cumplimiento.control_aml, registro_cumplimiento.terminos_wise_v4  Sample JSON records provided in the thread appear to show full names, dates of birth, verified Spanish national ID numbers (NIF/NIE), validated phone numbers, email addresses, and geographic locations. The data also allegedly includes Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) verification statuses.  Wise, formerly known as TransferWise, is a prominent financial technology company that facilitates cross-border payments and currency exchange for millions of users worldwide. If authentic, the exposure of verified identity documents and compliance records could facilitate identity theft, financial fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns against Spanish customers.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and Wise has not issued a public statement regarding the incident.  #DataBreach #Wise #DataSale #FinanceSecurity #Spain #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 Alleged Sale of Fortinet VPN Access on a Dark Web Forum  It has been discovered that a threat actor codenamed “SantaAd” is offering Fortinet VPN credentials for sale on a popular dark web forum. The listing claims to contain approximately 34,000 sets of credentials, which are associated with 6,896 unique Fortinet VPN IP addresses, with more than 3,100 of them located in the United States. The threat actor claims that the credentials are up-to-date, that the exploited vulnerability has not yet been publicly disclosed, and that password hash dumps will also be provided to buyers.  It has been observed that the listing has been updated over time and the asking prices have been raised. In the initial posting, the starting price was set at 25,000 USD and the “Blitz” (instant purchase) price at 50,000 USD; in a subsequent update, the starting price was raised to 30,000 USD. In the most recent update, the starting price was listed as 60,000 USD, the bid increment as 25,000 USD, and the instant purchase price as 120,000 USD. Additionally, the threat actor claimed that access is still being obtained, citing recent security research affecting Fortinet firewalls. The accuracy of the claims made in the post, the nature of the mentioned security vulnerability, and the validity of the access credentials offered for sale have not been independently verified.  #Fortinet #VPN #access #sale #Darkweb #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 A New Information Stealer Has Been Identified: "Lich Stealer" (aka The Lich)  A stealer written in Python is being sold on an underground forum, claiming Chrome App Bound Encryption (V10/V11/V20+) bypass via LSASS identity impersonation.  Steals: browser creds, cookies, CC, 40+ wallet extensions, Discord tokens, FTP/VPN configs  Drops a clipper in System32 (XOR + Base64) hijacking 20+ crypto address types  Disables Defender, plus anti VM, anti debug, anti sandbox  Exfil via Telegram bot and Discord webhook (LOG.zip)  Persistence: HKCU...\Run + fake error decoy  #ThreatIntel #InfoStealer #Malware #Cybersecurity #ABE #Chrome #ThreatMon

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-20 07:52 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협은 AI 에이전트와 자동화된 공급망 공격을 중심으로 급격히 진화하고 있습니다. 특히 `AutoJack`과 같은 공격 기법이 등장하며 AI 브라우징 에이전트가 RCE(Remote Code Execution)의 매개체로 악용되고 있으며, `The Gentlemen`과 같은 RaaS 조직은 EDR을 무력화하는 전용 프레임워크를 배포하는 등 방어 체계를 정면으로 돌파하고 있습니다. 또한, `FortiBleed` 캠페인처럼 대규모 자격 증명 탈취가 지속되고 있어, 기업의 ID 및 비밀번호 관리 체계의 근본적인 전환이 요구되는 시점입니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **The Gentlemen (Krebs on Security, WeLiveSecurity):** EDR 무력화 프레임워크인 `GentleKiller`를 활용해 보안 프로세스를 공격하는 RaaS 조직.
- **Kimsuky (이스트시큐리티):** 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격을 통해 국내 보안 담당자를 타겟팅.
- **FishMonger (WeLiveSecurity):** `SprySOCKS` 백도어와 커널 드라이버를 활용해 macOS 환경에서 고도의 은밀성을 유지하는 공격 수행.
- **TeamPCP (CyberScoop):** 오픈소스 소프트웨어의 신뢰 모델을 악용하여 공급망 공격을 주도하는 위협 그룹.
- **Icarus (BleepingComputer):** `Klue`의 OAuth 토큰을 탈취하여 고객사의 Salesforce 환경에 무단 접근하는 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **Kimsuky:** 국내 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출 문의를 가장한 스피어 피싱 공격이 확인됨.
- **공급망 공격:** `chai-as-init`이라는 악성 npm 패키지가 유명 테스트 프레임워크인 `Chai.js`의 플러그인으로 위장하여 배포됨.
- **기타:** 안랩 ASEC 보고서에 따르면 5월 국내 금융권 대상 피싱 공격이 2025년 12월 이후 최고치를 기록함.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-50195:** `containerd`, Image Cache Poisoning via CRI Checkpoint Import, Critical.
- **CVE-2026-53489:** `containerd`, Arbitrary Host File Read via Symlink Following, High.
- **CVE-2026-54527:** `JupyterLab-git`, Stored XSS leading to RCE, High.
- **CVE-2026-53492:** `containerd`, Improper Input Validation, High.
- **CVE-2026-50559:** `Quarkus`, HTTP path-based authorization bypass, Medium/High.

### 5. IOC 요약
- **도메인:** `nab-gov[.]kz` ([askardyuss](https://twitter.com/askardyuss/status/2067681510887735359))
- **해시:** (이번 수집에서 직접적인 파일 해시값은 제공되지 않음)
- **CVE:** `CVE-2026-50195`, `CVE-2026-53489`, `CVE-2026-54527`, `CVE-2026-53492`, `CVE-2026-50559` ([dailycve](https://twitter.com/dailycve/status/2068099508710363616))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **자격 증명 관리:** `FortiBleed` 캠페인 피해를 방지하기 위해 모든 Fortinet 장비의 관리자 및 VPN 자격 증명을 즉시 교체하고 MFA를 강제 적용하십시오.
- **공급망 보안:** 개발 환경에서 사용 중인 `npm` 패키지 중 `chai-as-init`과 같은 의심스러운 의존성을 전수 조사하고 최신 버전으로 업데이트하십시오.
- **AI 에이전트 통제:** 사내 도입된 AI 에이전트가 외부 웹 페이지를 로드하거나 코드를 실행할 수 있는 권한을 최소화하고, `AutoJack`과 같은 공격 시나리오에 대비한 샌드박스 환경을 구축하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수:** 8.5/10
- **근거:** AI 에이전트 취약점과 EDR 무력화 도구가 결합되어 공격의 자동화 및 정교화가 최고조에 달했으며, 대규모 자격 증명 유출이 지속되고 있음.
